steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage contrasts high-altitude studies (the blank) with below-sea-level studies, which have received "comparatively little notice." The contrast word "though" signals the blank must be the opposite of little-studied: abundant.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean abundant or plentiful to contrast with "comparatively little notice." Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — preliminary: early-stage or not yet complete — this suggests the high-altitude studies are just beginning, not that they are numerous. ✗
  • Choice B — copious: large in quantity, abundant — copious studies of high altitude contrast perfectly with the scant attention to below-sea-level effects. ✓
  • Choice C — equivocal: ambiguous or unclear in meaning — this describes the quality of findings, not the quantity of studies. ✗
  • Choice D — sporadic: occurring irregularly and infrequently — this is similar to "little notice," not opposite to it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
